tldr: Move mobile push and in-app messaging from Airship to Batch by re-instrumenting SDKs, re-importing user data, and rebuilding segments and campaigns.
why_switch: EU/GDPR data residency posture often cited for Batch
Freemium entry vs Airship's paid-only subscription
Lighter, mobile-first focus if you don't need email/SMS in one suite
Simpler campaign UI for teams primarily running push and in-app
Reduce vendor lock-in tied to Airship's audience model
what_to_export: User profiles with stable external/customer IDs
Historical message logs for reference (if needed)
export_method: Use Airship's data export and reporting capabilities to extract user, device, tag, and event data; consult Airship's official documentation for the supported export pathways and any required permissions. For campaign and template content, copy from the dashboard or the Airship API where available. Store exports as CSV/JSON for transformation before import.
import_method: Install the Batch mobile SDKs in your iOS and Android apps following Batch's official integration docs, then map your user identifiers and attributes to Batch's user/custom-data model. Recreate segments, push templates, and in-app campaigns in Batch's dashboard. Use Batch's APIs (transactional and data) per their documentation to backfill any user attributes and trigger transactional sends.

concept_mapping.note: Stable external ID for cross-device identity
Re-model boolean tags as attributes or audience rules
Re-emit from app code via Batch SDK

gotchas: Device tokens must be re-collected via the new SDK; legacy tokens may not be transferable
Users will receive duplicate pushes during the dual-run period if not gated
Segment logic rarely maps 1:1 — expect manual rebuild and QA
Consent and opt-in state must be migrated carefully to stay GDPR-compliant
Web push and email/SMS coverage in Batch is narrower than Airship's full suite
Pricing scales with MAUs/sends — model costs before committing

email.subject: Migrating from Airship to Batch — plan and timeline
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning a migration of our mobile push and in-app messaging stack from Airship to Batch. The goal is to consolidate on an EU-friendly mobile-first platform and simplify our campaign tooling.

High-level plan:
1) Export user profiles, device metadata, tags, and campaign content from Airship.
2) Integrate Batch SDKs into iOS and Android, mapping our external IDs and attributes.
3) Rebuild segments, templates, and triggered campaigns in Batch.
4) Run both platforms in parallel for one release cycle, then cut over.

Estimated effort: 2-6 weeks depending on campaign volume. I'll share a detailed checklist and risks (duplicate sends, consent state, segment parity) in the planning doc.

Thanks
